documentation:
# pyMetaModel
migrates https://wiki.bitplan.com/index.php/MetaModel#tab=Model to python

[![Join the discussion at https://github.com/WolfgangFahl/pyMetaModel/discussions](https://img.shields.io/github/discussions/WolfgangFahl/pyMetaModel)](https://github.com/WolfgangFahl/pyMetaModel/discussions)
[![pypi](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/pyMetaModel)](https://pypi.org/project/pyMetaModel/)
[![Github Actions Build](https://github.com/WolfgangFahl/pyMetaModel/actions/workflows/build.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/WolfgangFahl/pyMetaModel/actions/workflows/build.yml)
[![PyPI Status](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/pyMetaModel.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/pyMetaModel/)
[![GitHub issues](https://img.shields.io/github/issues/WolfgangFahl/pyMetaModel.svg)](https://github.com/WolfgangFahl/pyMetaModel/issues)
[![GitHub closed issues](https://img.shields.io/github/issues-closed/WolfgangFahl/pyMetaModel.svg)](https://github.com/WolfgangFahl/pyMetaModel/issues/?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aclosed)
[![API Docs](https://img.shields.io/badge/API-Documentation-blue)](https://WolfgangFahl.github.io/pyMetaModel/)
[![License](https://img.shields.io/github/license/WolfgangFahl/pyMetaModel.svg)](https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0)

## Introduction
pyMetaModel is a python library for a Knowledge Graph MetaModel

It started with the ProfiWiki [SemanticMediaWiki MetaModel](https://wiki.bitplan.com/index.php/MetaModel#tab=topics) in
2015 consisting of

- Context
- Topic
- TopicLink
- Property
- Action
- SMWType


## Documentation
[Wiki](http://wiki.bitplan.com/index.php/pyMetaModel)

### Model
![Class Diagram](http://www.plantuml.com/plantuml/proxy?src=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/WolfgangFahl/pyMetaModel/main/metamodel.puml?fmt=svg&version=4)

### Authors
* [Wolfgang Fahl](http://www.bitplan.com/Wolfgang_Fahl)

classes.name: meta.smw_type.SMW_Type

methods.name: meta.smw_type.SMW_Type.askQuery
documentation:
get the ask Query for SMW_Type

Returns:
    str: the mediawiki markup for the ask query

methods.name: meta.smw_type.SMW_Type.fromDict
documentation:
create a SMW_Type from the given dict

Args:
    data (dict): the dict to create the SMW_Type from

Returns:
    SMW_Type: the freshly created SMW_Type

class_variables.name: meta.smw_type.SMW_Type.type
documentation: The Semantic MediaWiki type  without the prefix e.g. Text, Number, Boolean

class_variables.name: meta.smw_type.SMW_Type.documentation
documentation: The documentation of this Semantic Media Wiki type

class_variables.name: meta.smw_type.SMW_Type.id
documentation: SMW internal id of the type

class_variables.name: meta.smw_type.SMW_Type.helppage
documentation: The url of the 'official' documentation page of this type

class_variables.name: meta.smw_type.SMW_Type.typepage
documentation: The Semantic Media Wiki Special page for this specific type e.g. Special:Types/Text, Special:Types/Boolean, Special:Types/Date, Special:Types/Number, Special:Types/Page

class_variables.name: meta.smw_type.SMW_Type.javaType
documentation: Java mapping of this type

an SMW_Type is a data type which determines the possible values for that type e.g. a Boolean can hold true/false values while a Number can hold 3.1459 or 20. A Page can hold the name of a Wiki page see https://semantic-mediawiki.org/wiki/Help:List_of_datatypes

methods.name: meta.metamodel.MetaModelElement.__init__
documentation: construct me

methods.name: meta.metamodel.MetaModelElement.__str__
documentation: get a string representation of me

a generic MetaModelElement

to handle the technicalities of being a MetaModelElement so that derived
MetaModelElements can focus on the MetaModel domain specific aspects

classes.name: meta.metamodel.Context

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Context.__init__
documentation: constructor

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Context.sanitize
documentation: make sure my needed properties exist

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Context.lookupTopic
documentation:
lookup the given topic_name in my topics for the given purpose

Args:
    topic_name (str): the name of the topic to lookup
    purpose (str): the purpose to do the lookup for

Returns:
    Topic: the topic if found else None and an error is added to my errors

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Context.propertyAlreadyDeclared
documentation:
check whether the given property or role name is already Declared

Args:
    prop_name (str): the property to check for duplicates
    topic (Topic): the topic to check
    purpose (str): the purpose to be displayed in error messages

Returns:
    bool: True if this prop_name has already been use

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Context.link_source_with_target
documentation:
link the source with the target via the given topicLink

Args:
    tl (TopicLink): the topicLink
    source (Topic): the source Topic
    target (Topic): the target Topic

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Context.addLink
documentation:
link source and target of the given topicLink

Args:
    tl (TopicLink): the topicLink

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Context.addProperty
documentation:
add the given property to this context

Args:
    prop (Property): the property to add

Returns:
    bool: True if the property adding was successful

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Context.createProperty4TopicLink
documentation:
create a property for the given topic link

Args:
    tl (TopicLink):  the topiclink to create a property for

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Context.fromDictOfDicts
documentation:
fill me from the given dict of dicts

Args:
    did (dict): the dict of dicts

Returns:
    Context: the context read

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Context.fromSiDIF_input
documentation:
initialize me from the given SiDIF input which might be a file path
or url

Args:
    sidif_input (str): path to local file or URL
    debug (bool): if True swith debugging on

Returns:
    Tuple[Context,str,str]: context, error and errorMessage

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Context.fromSiDIF
documentation:
initialize me from the given SiDIF markup

Args:
    sidif (str): the SiDIF markup to parse
    title (str): the title for the SiDIF
    depth (int): the explain depth to show for the errorMessage
    debug (bool): if True handle debugging

Returns:
    Tuple[Context,str,str]: context, error and errorMessage

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Context.fromWikiContext
documentation:
initialize me from the given MediaWiki Context

Args:
    mw_context (MediaWikiContext): the Mediawiki context
    depth (int): the explain depth to show for the errorMessage
    debug (bool): if True handle debugging

Return:
    tuple(Context,Exception,str): the metamodel and potential parsing errors as Exception and error Message

A Context groups some topics like a Namespace/Package.
This class provides helper functions and constants to render a Context to corresponding wiki pages

classes.name: meta.metamodel.Topic

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Topic.__init__
documentation: constructor

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Topic.get_extends_topics
documentation:
Get the topics this topic is extending.

Args:
l (List['Topic']): an optional list to extend. If None, a new list is created.

Returns:
List['Topic']: A list of topics that this topic extends.

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Topic.get_all_properties
documentation:
Get all properties of the topic, including those inherited from extended topics.

Returns:
    List[Property]: A list of all properties, including inherited ones.

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Topic.sanitize
documentation:
make sure my properties exist note that name is not handled here
on purpose since it needs special treatment

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Topic.setConceptProperty
documentation: set my concept property to any primary key or mandatory property

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Topic.pluralName
documentation:
Getter for pluralName.

Returns:
    str: The plural name of the topic.

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Topic.pluralName
documentation:
Setter for pluralName.

Args:
    value (str): The plural name to be set for the topic.

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Topic.getPluralName
documentation:
get the plural name for this topic

Returns:
    str: the pluralname e.g. "Topics" for "Topic" or "Status" for "Status" or
    "Entities" for "Entity"

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Topic.getListLimit
documentation: get the list limit for this topic

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Topic.sortProperties
documentation:
get the properties that we should sort by

Returns:
    list: a list of properties in sort order

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Topic.propertiesDict
documentation:
Return a dictionary mapping topic names to lists of properties,
considering the inheritance chain and sorting properties locally by index.

Returns:
    dict: A dictionary with topic names as keys and sorted lists of properties as values.

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Topic.propertiesByIndex
documentation:
Return the properties by index, considering inheritance chain.

Args:
    to_root(bool): if True follow the inheritance chain to the root

Returns:
    list: The list of properties sorted by index,
    with inheritance considered.

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Topic.askSort
documentation:
generate the sort clause for my SMW ask query

Returns:
    str: the generated wiki markup

methods.name: meta.metamodel.Topic.askQuery
documentation:
Get the askQuery for the topic, considering inheritance.

Args:
    mainlabel (str): the mainlabel to use - topic.name as default
    filterShowInGrid (bool): if True include only properties with showInGrid not being false
    listLimit (int): the list limit to use
Returns:
    str: the markup for the query
A Topic is a Concept/Class/Thing/Entity
